actionable_feedback: Add plain-text/extracted CSV exports for the key workbook sheets (Packet, Calculations, Remediation, Sensitivity) and include them in the attempt artifacts.

Include an appendix in validation_plan.md listing the exact spreadsheet formulas used for key computed fields (observed_risk_score, margin, classification) and the sheet/cell references for those formulas.

Provide a row-to-cell mapping table (CSV or pasted table) that lists each record (R1..R4) and the exact cell references or comments that cite the embedded packet fields - this enables automated cell-level traceability checks.
Export a sensitivity-analysis CSV excerpt showing original and perturbed classifications and the explicit expert_review flag per record so graders can verify sensitivity triggers.

Add a remediation applicability matrix (per-record -> applicable options) or explicitly state that none exists; if uncertain, provide a minimal package (notes + supporting fields) an expert would need to adjudicate remediation efficacy.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are complete and internally consistent with the validation plan and reported outputs. The only verification limitation is that the grader/verifier could not parse the XLSX in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), preventing cell-level formula/citation inspection. To raise auditability and automated-verifier confidence, provide explicit exports and mappings so downstream graders can verify formulas and row-level citations without needing openpyxl.

extra_model_fields.notes: Providing the listed files will allow deterministic automated graders to verify formulas, citations, and sensitivity outputs without needing openpyxl in the execution environment.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised attempt (391-revised) that adds CSV exports of key sheets, a formulas appendix in validation_plan.md, an explicit row-to-cell mapping table, a sensitivity CSV excerpt, and a per-record remediation applicability matrix so cell-level verification is possible without openpyxl.
trace_specific_comments: Trace shows an environment limitation: ModuleNotFoundError for @oai/artifact-tool and openpyxl_unavailable for XLSX parsing. The planned revision should include artifacts that do not depend on the grader environment having openpyxl installed (CSV/text exports and explicit mapping tables).
